Provenance.drafts: LW-art parity, 2026-07-19: round 1, 4 candidates (seed 497501341+0..+3) at 1024x1024 via ComfyUI MCP -- all 4 rejected: every one drew a full 3/4-isometric room corner (walls, floor, sometimes a window) with shelves already loaded with pottery/jars/barrels, violating both the 'flat front elevation' and 'empty display slots' requirements. Escalated: round 2, 4 more candidates (seed+4..+7) with the subject rewritten to explicitly demand a flat 2D front-elevation shelf-wall texture (no perspective/room-corner/floor/ceiling/window) and completely bare shelves, plus matching negatives (isometric, room corner, perspective, vanishing point, pottery, jars, jugs, barrels, baskets, crates, cluttered shelves). This produced 3 clean, empty, front-on repeating shelf-row textures out of 4.
Provenance.paintoverNote: Winner is round-2 c0 (seed+4, resolved 497501345): a clean repeating flat shelf-wall texture, warm honey-amber lit, completely bare cubby rows, no perspective, no clutter, no text/frame artifacts. Generated at the full 1024x1024 Active-track square (matching every other backdrop/building precedent) then manually cropped to a 1024x224 horizontal band (y=[440,664]) centered on two clean shelf-lip rows -- chosen over the raw non-uniform runtime squash ShopStage.cs would otherwise apply, so the committed asset is already close to its final ~1024x220 display aspect and the wood grain doesn't read flattened.
Provenance.aiDisclosure: AI-generated (SDXL base 1.0) diffuse. DEVIATION from ShopSpecs.cs's docstring (which anticipated the normal BiRefNet-cutout chain): the winning candidate is a full-bleed, edge-to-edge shelf texture with no isolated background region for BiRefNet's salient-object segmentation to remove (the whole frame IS the subject, the same condition that makes mine-backdrop/gloomwood-backdrop skip cutout.py) -- cutout.py was skipped and the crop was format-converted straight to opaque RGBA instead. Normal map still derived via normalmap.py (Sobel height-from-luminance, strength 2.5) since NormalMap=true and the surface is a near, lit, structured wall (not a receding atmospheric plane); Flip-Y-OFF import convention unchanged.
